questions.question: Simplify: (3 + 2i)(2 - i)
questions.explanation: Use FOIL: 3·2 + 3·(-i) + 2i·2 + 2i·(-i) = 6 - 3i + 4i - 2i². Since i² = -1, this becomes 6 + i + 2 = 8 + i.

questions.question: What is i^47?
questions.explanation: Powers of i repeat every 4 terms. Divide 47 by 4: 47 = 11·4 + 3, so i^47 = i³ = -i.

questions.question: Express (5 + 4i)/(1 - i) in the form a + bi.
questions.explanation: Multiply numerator and denominator by the conjugate (1 + i): [(5 + 4i)(1 + i)] / [(1 - i)(1 + i)] = (5 + 5i + 4i + 4i²) / (1 - i²) = (1 + 9i) / 2 = 1/2 + 9i/2.

questions.question: If z = 2 - 3i, what is z · z̄ (the product of z and its complex conjugate)?
questions.explanation: The conjugate of 2 - 3i is 2 + 3i. Their product is (2 - 3i)(2 + 3i) = 4 - 9i² = 4 + 9 = 13.

questions.question: If (a + bi) + (3 - 2i) = 5 + i, find a + b.
questions.explanation: Equate real and imaginary parts: a + 3 = 5 and b - 2 = 1. So a = 2 and b = 3, giving a + b = 5.
